name: asciitree
documentation:
# ASCII Trees

```console
asciitree
 +-- sometimes
 |   +-- you
 +-- just
 |   +-- want
 |       +-- to
 |       +-- draw
 +-- trees
 +-- in
     +-- your
         +-- terminal
```

```python
from asciitree import LeftAligned
from collections import OrderedDict as OD

tree = {
    'asciitree': OD([
        ('sometimes',
            {'you': {}}),
        ('just',
            {'want': OD([
                ('to', {}),
                ('draw', {}),
            ])}),
        ('trees', {}),
        ('in', {
            'your': {
                'terminal': {}
            }
        })
    ])
}

tr = LeftAligned()
print(tr(tree))
```

Read the documentation at <http://pythonhosted.org/asciitree>

classes.name: asciitree.traversal.Traversal

methods.name: asciitree.traversal.Traversal.get_children
documentation: Return a list of children of a node.

methods.name: asciitree.traversal.Traversal.get_root
documentation: Return a node representing the tree root from the tree.

methods.name: asciitree.traversal.Traversal.get_text
documentation: Return the text associated with a node.

Traversal method.

Used by the tree rendering functions like {class}`~asciitree.LeftAligned`.

classes.name: asciitree.traversal.DictTraversal
documentation:
Traversal suitable for a dictionary. Keys are tree labels, all values
must be dictionaries as well.

classes.name: asciitree.traversal.AttributeTraversal

class_variables.name: asciitree.traversal.AttributeTraversal.attribute
documentation: : Attribute to use.

Attribute traversal.

Uses an attribute of a node as its list of children.

modules.name: asciitree
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: asciitree.draw_tree
documentation:
Support asciitree 0.2 API.

This function solely exist to not break old code (using asciitree 0.2).
Its use is deprecated.

classes.name: asciitree.LeftAligned

methods.name: asciitree.LeftAligned.render
documentation:
Renders a node. This function is used internally, as it returns
a list of lines. Use {func}`~asciitree.LeftAligned.__call__` instead.

methods.name: asciitree.LeftAligned.__call__
documentation:
Render the tree into string suitable for console output.

```
param tree

A tree.
```

Creates a renderer for a left-aligned tree.

Any attributes of the resulting class instances can be set using
constructor arguments.

Like {func}`~asciitree.drawing.Style.child_head` but only called
for the last child.
